extraction.one_line_summary: BARGUIL won from breakaway while FROOME survived AG2R pressure after mechanical, LANDA sacrificing GC position to pace leader back.
tactical_patterns: FROOME mechanical prompted immediate AG2R acceleration with BARDET attacking, proving no neutralization when team already committed to offensive.
LANDA dropped from sixth to behind MARTIN in GC by pacing FROOME back, definitively proving Sky single-leader strategy.
MARTIN bridged to dropped breakaway riders who cooperated despite GC threat, suggesting personal relationships override tactical logic late-tour.
Sky exhausted entire team roster pacing FROOME across 40-second gap on penultimate Cat 1, leaving zero support for final climb.
YATES attacked GC group late despite stage being decided, signaling third-week form building for Alps as directeur predicted.

rider_signals.FROOME Chris: neutral: closed 40-second gap after mechanical using entire team, but exposed vulnerability with zero domestiques remaining for Alps
rider_signals.LANDA Mikel: negative: sacrificed own GC position (dropped from 6th to 7th behind MARTIN) to pace leader, confirms no personal ambitions
rider_signals.BARDET Romain: positive: AG2R immediately attacked FROOME mechanical without hesitation, showing predatory race reading and team commitment to offense
rider_signals.MARTIN Dan: positive: jumped to 5th GC (1:12 down) by bridging to breakaway remnants while still managing crash injuries
rider_signals.YATES Simon: positive: attacked GC group on final climb despite stage decided, demonstrating improving form for third week Alps
rider_signals.BARGUIL Warren: positive: won solo from breakaway, timing split from original group perfectly to hold off MARTIN's late charge
